message: Spec created from roadmap feature feature-1
Build orchestration started and worktree prepared.
Permission profile loaded (default)
Planning completed and implementation subtasks were generated.

Started subtask 1/2: Add styles for a .privacy-badge component inside the existing <style> block. Style it as a small, non-intrusive banner below the tagline: subtle background (e.g. light green tint), small font, inline icon characters (no external icon fonts), flex layout for the three claim pills (no cookies · no tracking · no account), and a 'View Source' anchor. Include a @media (max-width: 480px) rule to stack or wrap the pills on narrow viewports.

Style it as a small, non-intrusive banner below the tagline: subtle background (e.g. light green tint), small font, inline icon characters (no external icon fonts), flex layout for the three claim pills (no cookies · no tracking · no account), and a 'View Source' anchor. Include a @media (max-width: 480px) rule to stack or wrap the pills on narrow viewports.

message:
Done. Here's a summary of what was changed and validated:

**Changes made** (`index.html`, inside `<style>`):

- Added `.privacy-badge` — flex, wrap, centered, light green tint (`#f0faf2`) background with a `#c3e6cb` border, `0.78rem` font, `#2d6a3f` text, `8px` border-radius and `border: 1px solid` for a subtle card feel.
- Added `.privacy-badge .privacy-pills` — nested flex/wrap container to hold the claim tokens in a row that can wrap.
- Added `.privacy-badge .pill` with a CSS `::before` `·` separator between pills (no extra markup needed, pure CSS).
- Added `.privacy-badge .view-source` — styled as an underlined, bold anchor in the same green palette.
- Extended the existing `@media (max-width: 480px)` block with rules that switch `.privacy-badge` to `flex-direction: column` and tighten pill gaps on narrow viewports.

**Validation:** The styles are self-contained (no external fonts or icon fonts — only Unicode emoji characters will be used by the next subtask for the icons), responsive at 375px+, and non-intrusive in size and color. No third-party resources were added.

Completed subtask 1/2: Add styles for a .privacy-badge component inside the existing <style> block. Style it as a small, non-intrusive banner below the tagline: subtle background (e.g. light green tint), small font, inline icon characters (no external icon fonts), flex layout for the three claim pills (no cookies · no tracking · no account), and a 'View Source' anchor. Include a @media (max-width: 480px) rule to stack or wrap the pills on narrow viewports.

message: Started subtask 2/2: Insert a <div class="privacy-badge"> element in the .container, immediately after the .tagline paragraph. The badge must contain three visible claim tokens — '🔒 No cookies', '🚫 No tracking', '👤 No account required' — and a 'View Source' link using the view-source: protocol (view-source: + location.href rendered via a small inline script attribute or a static relative path) so users can inspect the full HTML in-browser and verify the zero-external-requests guarantee themselves.

message:
Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of what was changed and validated:

**Changed:** Inserted a `<div class="privacy-badge">` element in `index.html` immediately after the `.tagline` paragraph (line 292). It contains:
- Three claim pills: `🔒 No cookies`, `🚫 No tracking`, `👤 No account required`
- A `View Source` anchor with `id="view-source-link"` initially pointing to `#`
- An inline `<script>` tag immediately after the badge that sets the link's `href` to `view-source:` + `location.href` at runtime, so it always points to the current page's source regardless of where the file is served from

**Validated:** The styles for `.privacy-badge`, `.privacy-pills`, `.pill`, `.view-source`, and the `@media (max-width: 480px)` responsive rules were already present in the `<style>` block from a prior subtask, so the badge will render correctly without any additional CSS work needed.

Completed subtask 2/2: Insert a <div class="privacy-badge"> element in the .container, immediately after the .tagline paragraph. The badge must contain three visible claim tokens — '🔒 No cookies', '🚫 No tracking', '👤 No account required' — and a 'View Source' link using the view-source: protocol (view-source: + location.href rendered via a small inline script attribute or a static relative path) so users can inspect the full HTML in-browser and verify the zero-external-requests guarantee themselves.

message: Automated AI review started.

All acceptance criteria are satisfied. Here is the verdict:

```json
{
  "status": "approved",
  "summary": "Privacy badge implemented correctly: visible without interaction, communicates all three zero-tracking claims, includes a working view-source link, no external resources added, and responsive layout handles both mobile and desktop viewports.",
  "issues": [],
  "validations": [
    "Privacy badge is rendered directly in the HTML with no user interaction required",
    "Three claim pills present: '🔒 No cookies', '🚫 No tracking', '👤 No account required'",
    "View Source link uses view-source: protocol set via inline script (document.getElementById('view-source-link').href = 'view-source:' + location.href)",
    "No external scripts, CDN fonts, analytics, or tracking resources were introduced",
    "Responsive CSS: flex-wrap on pills and a @media (max-width: 480px) block stacks the badge to column layout on narrow viewports",
    "Badge uses subtle light-green tint (#f0faf2 background, #c3e6cb border) — non-intrusive styling consistent with the spec",
    "Badge is positioned immediately after the .tagline paragraph as specified in the subtasks"
  ]
}
```
AI review completed. Awaiting human review and PR decision.

validations: Privacy badge is rendered directly in the HTML with no user interaction required
Three claim pills present: '🔒 No cookies', '🚫 No tracking', '👤 No account required'
View Source link uses view-source: protocol set via inline script (document.getElementById('view-source-link').href = 'view-source:' + location.href)
No external scripts, CDN fonts, analytics, or tracking resources were introduced
Responsive CSS: flex-wrap on pills and a @media (max-width: 480px) block stacks the badge to column layout on narrow viewports
Badge uses subtle light-green tint (#f0faf2 background, #c3e6cb border) — non-intrusive styling consistent with the spec
Badge is positioned immediately after the .tagline paragraph as specified in the subtasks

message:
All CSS rules are already implemented and meet the subtask requirements:

- **`.privacy-badge`**: light-green tint (`#f0faf2`), border (`#c3e6cb`), flex layout with `flex-wrap: wrap`, small font (`0.78rem`), green text (`#2d6a3f`)
- **`.privacy-pills`**: flex row with `flex-wrap: wrap` and `gap`
- **`.pill + .pill::before`**: dot separator (`content: '·'`) with margin
- **`.view-source`**: underlined, `font-weight: 600`, matching green (`#2d6a3f`)
- **`@media (max-width: 480px)`**: `.privacy-badge` switches to `flex-direction: column`, `.privacy-pills` gap tightened to `4px 6px`

The HTML markup and inline script for the `view-source:` link are also already present at lines 292–300.

No changes were needed — the subtask was fully implemented in the prior commit (`2bc617e`). Validation confirmed all rules are in place and structurally correct.
